subject: Biology
title: Invasive Species
description: A micro-lesson that makes Invasive Species usable.

hook.text: When a species arrives somewhere new with no predators, it can multiply out of control.

buildup.text: Cane toads were brought to Australia to eat beetles. They ate everything else instead.

discovery.text: Invasive species outcompete natives for food and space, often driving them toward extinction.

twist.text: Burmese pythons released in Florida's Everglades have decimated native mammal populations.

climax.text: Invasive species cost the global economy over $400 billion a year in damage and control efforts.

punchline.text: Wrong species, wrong place, massive damage.

quiz.question: Why are invasive species so harmful to new environments?
options: They lack natural predators and outcompete natives
